<!--
Thanks for opening a PR. Please fill in the sections below so reviewers can act on it quickly.
If you are adding a new entry, please also confirm the criteria self-check.
-->

## What does this PR do?

<!-- One-liner. e.g. "Add owner/repo to Skill collections." -->

## If adding a new entry

**Repo URL:** <https://github.com/owner/repo>

**One-line description of what it ships:**

**Primary shape category:** <!-- e.g. Skill collections, MCP servers, Behaviour-modification skills > Output-discipline -->

**Tool / ecosystem tag(s):** <!-- e.g. claude-code, cursor, multi-tool, mcp -->

**Install mechanism:** <!-- e.g. copy, script, npm, marketplace, plugin, extension -->

**Licence:** <!-- SPDX identifier or "none" -->

### Criteria self-check

- [ ] I've read [CRITERIA.md](../CRITERIA.md).
- [ ] The repo's primary deliverable is installable context for AI agents (not a product that uses AI internally).
- [ ] There's a clear install / consume path that brings the content into the consumer's AI-agent workflow.
- [ ] The content is structured for agent consumption (not free-form human-only docs).
- [ ] The repo is public and has a licence (or I've flagged "no licence").
- [ ] The repo isn't itself an awesome-list (those are linked under "Other discovery resources," not listed as entries).
- [ ] Description doesn't use marketing language ("blazing fast", "next-generation", "the best").

### Format check

- [ ] Entry uses regular hyphen with spaces ` - ` (not em-dash, not en-dash).
- [ ] Description starts with an uppercase letter and ends with a period.
- [ ] URL ends with `#readme`.
- [ ] Entry placed in the correct shape-category section per [CATEGORIES.md](../CATEGORIES.md).
- [ ] Entry sorted alphabetically within its section (case-insensitive on the name).

## If updating an existing entry

<!-- Describe the change and why. -->
